title: Remote model-check fails OPEN: fold-back marks wrapper-omitted models installed:true
description:
SYMPTOM (observed on 1.1.1, NOT reproduced on 1.2): connected a CPU download-mode Pod (image v0.14.0-cpu) to install ONE model (Chroma Flash); instead 5 unrelated image models (SDXL Realistic, SDXL NSFW, ILL Anime, ILL Anime Beauty, PONY Mix) flipped to 'installed' one-by-one. Volume disk moved only +4.9GB (a real 6-model download is 30GB+), and the volume actually held Qwen/KREA from 1.2 - so NOTHING was downloaded; the models were FALSELY reported installed. The Pod wrapper had just 404'd twice ('remote models/check deferred - still booting').

ROOT (code-level, fail-open in the remote check adapter, routes/remoteModels.js remoteModelsCheck): line ~325 `const entry = results[mid] || { installed: true, deps: [] }` defaults any model the wrapper OMITS from its response to installed:true; line ~339 `entry.installed = deps.every((d) => d.installed)` is VACUOUSLY TRUE for an empty deps array. So when the wrapper returns partial/empty status (booting race, CPU-image quirk, or a {type,filename} dep-split mismatch that drops deps), the omitted models are marked installed with zero files present. The check fails OPEN (says installed when unsure) instead of CLOSED.

VERIFIED: the exact lines are PRESENT in the 1.2.0 tree (routes/remoteModels.js:325 and :339) - NOT removed by the MPI-276 download refactor. Whether it TRIGGERS on 1.2 is unproven (user never saw the cascade on 1.2; depends on the wrapper ever returning partial results). Latent, unproven on 1.2.

FIX (small, safe): default wrapper-omitted models to installed:false (unknown != installed); guard the vacuous every -> `deps.length > 0 && deps.every(...)`.

ADJACENT: MPI-326 (the ~5s remote models:checked STORM) re-runs this same check every 5s while connected - so during Pod init the fail-open check re-fires repeatedly. Same code neighbourhood (syncModelInstalled -> remoteModelsCheck), different defect (326 = UI-storm/too-frequent; 328 = data-correctness/wrong-result). Fixing 326's edge-guard REDUCES how often 328 can fire but does NOT fix the fail-open.

OBSERVABILITY GAP (worth noting): the entire cascade produced ZERO app-level log lines - only [comfy] engine noise. remoteModelsCheck logs nothing about what the wrapper returned, so a real runaway leaves no trace. Consider logging the wrapper status-response shape.

USER DECISION PENDING: user is skeptical it affects 1.2 ('that never happened on 1.2') and wants the agent wrapping up 1.2 to judge whether to fix before Thursday or defer to 1.3.
